evals.name: notifications
evals.prompt: we need to add notification preferences per project — email, slack, or both. right now everyone gets email for everything
evals.expected_output: A complete spec with ACs covering: preference CRUD, per-project scoping, channel types (email/slack/both), default behavior, migration for existing users, UI for the preferences panel, notification delivery respecting preferences, edge cases (invalid channel, project deletion)
assertions: Spec contains a numbered acceptance criteria table with #, Criterion, and Level columns
Every AC has a valid test level (one of: unit, integration-db, integration-browser, integration-llm, e2e, visual)
Spec has at least 8 acceptance criteria
Spec has an Overview section explaining the feature purpose and mental model
Spec references specific files from the codebase (actual paths, not hypothetical)
Spec includes a Data Model section with table/column changes
ACs cover core happy path: user sets preference and notifications follow it
ACs cover at least one error/failure scenario
ACs cover edge cases (e.g. project deletion, no preference set = default behavior)
ACs explicitly address per-project scoping (not just global preferences)
ACs cover all three channel modes: email-only, slack-only, and both
Spec addresses migration from current behavior to new system
Spec does not prescribe test code, mock strategies, or specific function signatures

evals.name: action-items
evals.prompt: spec for: auto-extract action items from synced meetings and create tasks in the user's Linear workspace
evals.expected_output: A complete spec with ACs covering: LLM extraction from transcripts, task creation via Linear API, duplicate detection, mapping assignees, error handling (Linear auth failure, no transcript), extraction quality, UI for review/approval, toggle behavior
assertions: Spec contains a numbered acceptance criteria table with #, Criterion, and Level columns
Every AC has a valid test level
Spec has at least 8 acceptance criteria
Spec has an Overview section
Spec references specific files from the codebase
Spec includes a Data Model section with table/column changes
ACs cover LLM-based action item extraction from meeting transcripts
ACs cover task creation in Linear via API
ACs cover error scenarios (e.g. Linear auth failure, no transcript, extraction failure)
ACs address duplicate detection or re-extraction behavior
ACs cover UI for reviewing or managing extracted action items
Spec defines when extraction triggers (on sync? on demand? on enrichment completion?)
Spec does not prescribe test code, mock strategies, or specific function signatures

evals.name: bucket-prefixes
evals.prompt: the file upload stores everything in one bucket. we need workspace-scoped bucket prefixes and migrate existing files
evals.expected_output: A complete spec with ACs covering: new upload path with workspace prefix, read/serve from new paths, migration strategy for existing files, backwards compatibility during migration, RLS implications, error handling, rollback plan
assertions: Spec contains a numbered acceptance criteria table with #, Criterion, and Level columns
Every AC has a valid test level
Spec has at least 8 acceptance criteria
Spec has an Overview section
Spec references specific files from the codebase
Spec includes a Data Model or storage changes section
ACs cover new uploads going to workspace-scoped prefixes
ACs cover reading/serving files from the new paths
ACs cover migration strategy for existing files
ACs address backwards compatibility during migration
ACs cover error/edge cases (e.g. missing workspace, file not found during migration)
Spec addresses RLS or access control implications
Spec does not prescribe test code, mock strategies, or specific function signatures

evals.name: meeting-rules
evals.prompt: The current meeting scoping system is too noisy — it uses bootstrap mode, a ≥2 project members algorithm, and a blocklist. We need to replace all of that with user-defined inclusion rules. Two types: people rules (match meetings by attendee emails, with two groups for 'A met with B' patterns) and topic rules (match meetings by subject matter using LLM). Rules should be additive — each rule can only include, never exclude. Manual overrides still work. When a match is uncertain (fuzzy name match or LLM unsure), the meeting should be flagged as 'unsure' and stay excluded until the user confirms.
evals.expected_output: A spec comparable to docs/specs/meeting-inclusion-rules.md — covering rules engine, evaluation logic, confidence model, data model, UI for rule management, migration from old system, re-evaluation semantics, and comprehensive ACs
assertions: Spec contains a numbered acceptance criteria table with #, Criterion, and Level columns
Every AC has a valid test level
Spec has at least 15 acceptance criteria
Spec has an Overview section with exclude-by-default mental model
Spec references specific codebase files (meeting_scoping.py, fathom.py, meeting tables, etc.)
Spec includes a Data Model section with new tables and column changes
ACs cover people rule matching with both groups populated (A AND B)
ACs cover people rule matching with one empty group
ACs cover people rule with both groups empty (matches nothing)
ACs cover topic rule with confident LLM match → included
ACs cover topic rule with uncertain LLM match → unsure
ACs cover manual force-include and force-exclude overrides
ACs cover re-evaluation on rule changes and new meeting sync
Spec distinguishes high vs low confidence → included vs unsure
ACs cover rule deletion triggering re-evaluation
ACs cover additive rule semantics (union of matches)
Spec addresses removal of old system (bootstrap, ≥2 members, blocklist)
Spec includes UI section for rule management interface
Spec does not prescribe test code, mock strategies, or specific function signatures
